2026-07-14 08:59:15

Yesterday (Monday) - The market entered the new week on a relatively cautious note as investors continued evaluating recent gains against upcoming economic data and the start of earnings season. Pre-market futures were modestly lower, suggesting a softer opening, but TurboRegime remained focused on the broader trend rather than overnight sentiment. As always, futures provide context—not confirmation—and the strategy responds only to signals that remain intact at the closing bell.

Today (Tuesday) - The current RiskOn regime has now reached its eighth trading day. After briefly approaching a 1% gain late last week, the active trade has modestly retraced and is currently showing a return of 0.28%. Importantly, the maximum drawdown since entering the position remains just -0.22%, highlighting the relatively stable nature of this trade despite normal day-to-day market fluctuations.

Hold or Trade - HOLD. No portfolio changes are indicated at this time. Current allocation remains 85% SPY, 15% SHV, and 0% SPXL. The strategy continues to participate in the broader market while maintaining a modest defensive allocation until market momentum either strengthens enough to support a more aggressive posture or weakens sufficiently to warrant a defensive transition.

Current Risk Regime - TurboRegime remains in RiskOn, active since July 1. Although recent sessions have given back a portion of last week's gains, the overall character of the trade remains constructive. Temporary pullbacks are a normal part of any developing trend, and the model continues to prioritize confirmation over prediction. By requiring end-of-day confirmation before making allocation changes, TurboRegime seeks to avoid reacting to short-lived market noise while remaining positioned for longer-term opportunities.

Live Performance - Live tracking has now reached 104 days since beginning on April 1, 2026. During that period, TurboRegime has executed just six live trades while producing a cumulative return of 32.58%, compared with 14.66% for a buy-and-hold investment in SPY over the same period. Despite today's modest pullback, the strategy continues to outperform SPY by 17.92 percentage points.

General Commentary - One of the defining characteristics of TurboRegime is its willingness to remain patient. Rather than attempting to trade every market swing, the strategy waits for meaningful changes in market conditions before adjusting portfolio allocations. That disciplined approach has successfully navigated RiskOff, RiskOn, and RiskOn++ (Turbo) regimes during the first 104 days of live trading. While individual trades will naturally experience periods of both strength and weakness, the strategy remains focused on compounding returns over complete market cycles through consistent, objective decision-making.
